key_insight: A D-gun applied Al2O3-TiO2 coating significantly improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and a random forest model best predicts wear from test conditions.
engineering_relevance: The study links detonation-spray coating design with tribological performance, offering a practical route to extend titanium alloy life in aerospace, biomedical, and marine components.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spray.
Ran ASTM G99 pin-on-disc wear tests at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM showed reduced wear and tribo-layer formation on coated surfaces, while random forest had the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: The findings are based on a specific coating setup and test range, so model performance and wear gains may not generalize beyond the reported process parameters without further validation.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
